steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage notes that tool similarities could stem from imitation or trade, then presents a key finding from Altınışık et al.: "genetic affinity among the populations." The correct answer must use that genetic affinity evidence to support one specific conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'infers imitation patterns from genetic data': Genetic affinity shows biological connection, not imitation; this misuses the evidence. ✗
  • Choice B — 'uses genetics to establish migration order': The passage draws no conclusions about which group migrated first. ✗
  • Choice C — 'concludes populations were biologically connected, explaining similar tools without requiring imitation or trade': Genetic affinity directly supports population blending as the explanation, which the passage explicitly contrasts with imitation and trade. ✓
  • Choice D — 'introduces unsupported assumptions about cultural differences': The passage does not discuss cultural differences between the populations. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
